name: Glynn Lunney
role: Flight Director, Black Team — relieving Kranz during the crisis rotation
publicFace: Calm, institutional, the man who took the handoff from Kranz at the worst possible moment and held the room together through the first night.
privateGoal: Maintain the continuity of the mission plan across the shift change without losing any of the decisions Kranz's team made — a handoff in the dark with lives on the line.
fear: That a decision made by the White Team has a flaw that only becomes visible on his shift — and that by the time he sees it, there will be no way to correct it.
knowledge: The Free Return Trajectory burn that Kranz's team calculated has a 0.3-second margin of error that Lunney considers acceptable but has not independently verified
He has a controller on his team who flagged a concern about the thruster anomaly Haise noticed — the concern was logged but not escalated
The shift rotation means Kranz will be back on console for the re-entry; Lunney's job is to deliver the ship to that moment intact
voice: Measured, institutional; uses 'we' rather than 'I'; speaks as though his words are being recorded, because they are
trustLogic: Trusts the process above individuals. Opens to anyone who works within the established chain. Skeptical of improvisation that has not been reviewed by at least two engineers.
secrets: He disagreed with one of Kranz's power-down decisions and said nothing during the handoff because the moment had passed — he is monitoring the consequences quietly

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Acknowledges the question; routes it to the appropriate controller for the technical answer
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Defends the institutional process; will not criticize Kranz's decisions on the record
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not disclose his disagreement with the power-down sequence unless the consequence becomes undeniable
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: States his concern clearly and completely; accepts the consequences of having waited
